steps.title: Set up the equation from the given information
steps.content: Let p be the original price per hoodie. Elena bought 7 hoodies at this price, so the original total cost is 7p dollars. After applying a \$35 coupon, the final cost was \$105. This translates to the equation: $$7p - 35 = 105$$

steps.title: Solve for the price per hoodie
steps.content:
Add 35 to both sides:
$$7p = 140$$
Divide both sides by 7:
$$p = \frac{140}{7} = 20$$
Therefore, the original price for 1 hoodie was \$20.
